complaint_text: I keep waking up from nightmares that feel too real. Not every night, but enough that I’m scared to go to sleep. I wake up sweaty and my heart racing and it takes me ages to calm down.
decomp_notes: Decomposition into six changeable, current-state variables directly described or reasonably implied by the complaint (focused on nightmares, physiological arousal, sleep-related fear/avoidance, and difficulty resuming sleep).

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Awakens from nightmares on multiple nights (recurring but not nightly), producing repeated sleep interruption.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: "I keep waking up from nightmares... Not every night, but enough"
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Awakens from nightmares on multiple nights (recurring but not nightly), producing repeated sleep interruption.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Dream content is experienced as highly vivid and realistic, with strong subjective intensity.
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Dream content is experienced as highly vivid and realistic, with strong subjective intensity.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_label: Anticipatory fear of sleep / reluctance to go to bed
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Experiences fear or dread about going to sleep specifically because of expectation of nightmares, leading to avoidance or reluctance.
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Experiences fear or dread about going to sleep specifically because of expectation of nightmares, leading to avoidance or reluctance.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Upon awakening from nightmares, experiences marked physiological signs of arousal (e.g., sweating, rapid heart rate).
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: "I wake up sweaty and my heart racing"
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Upon awakening from nightmares, experiences marked physiological signs of arousal (e.g., sweating, rapid heart rate).

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: After awakening from a nightmare, requires an extended period to calm down and return to baseline arousal.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: "it takes me ages to calm down"
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: After awakening from a nightmare, requires an extended period to calm down and return to baseline arousal.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_label: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Has trouble falling back asleep after waking from a nightmare, due to lingering arousal or fear.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: Implied by combination of waking "sweaty and my heart racing" and that it "takes me ages to calm down" and being "scared to go to sleep."
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Has trouble falling back asleep after waking from a nightmare, due to lingering arousal or fear.

hyde_based_predictor_rankings.hyde_llm_summary: The case centers on recurrent nightmare-triggered awakenings with high perceived vividness, anticipatory sleep fear, and prolonged post-awakening arousal that disrupts returning to sleep. The proposed levers span psychological techniques for extinguishing fear and modifying nightmare content, autonomic regulation to lower arousal, sleep-environment and circadian alignment, social-context supports, and data-informed monitoring. Each lever is modifiable, non-pharmacological, and targets one or more of the core criteria (C01–C06) with clear measurement plans. Priorities emphasize high-impact, feasible psychoeducation and CBT-I–informed strategies (IRT, routine, relaxation), supplemented by physiological regulation (HRV biofeedback), grounding techniques, environmental optimization, social support, and measurement-based feedback to optimize adherence and track progress. Safety notes are included to escalate to professional care if distress worsens or risk emerges.

guidance.framework_context: The output of this script is part of a larger framework that will ultimately construct a criterion+predictor model that is ready for data collection (e.g., via ecological momentary assessments, digital phenotyping, etc.).The general treatment algorithm is based on breadth-first search algorithm that first explores the secondary nodes (i.e., child nodes of the primary nodes BIO PSYCHO and SOCIAL).After data collection, the most relevant solution-predictors are re-selected for further data-analyes where then I focus on their subsequent child nodes).This algorithm goes on until user reaches salutogenic satisfactory mental state.
guidance.goal: Construct an initial criterion+predictor observation model ready for data collection and suitable for later gVAR analysis. Preserve complaint resolution while remaining estimable.

requested_variable_counts.note: If 'choice', choose an optimal number for gVAR feasibility while preserving coverage. Hard cap: total nodes <= 18 (avoid dense grid truncation).

measurement_principles: Prefer 1–9 Likert (anchored) for many constructs to increase variance.
Avoid pure binary nodes; if needed, measure intensity 1–9 + optional event flag (not as node).
Use 'since last prompt' for momentary EMA; 'morning diary' for last-night sleep; 'end_of_day' for daily summaries.
If passive data used, specify precise signal + aggregation window.
gvar_constraints: Coherent design: prompts/day must match per-variable sampling_per_day for momentary variables.
Avoid redundancy/collinearity (pick one broad construct rather than near-duplicates).
Prefer variables expected to fluctuate within-person at chosen timescale.
Provide plausible lag bins aligned with schedule: '0-6h', '0-24h', '24-48h', 'multi_lag'.
pairwise_estimation_requirement: You MUST output three dense relevance arrays: FULL P->C, FULL directed P->P (i!=j), FULL directed C->C (i!=j).
Dense score format: comma decimal separator and exactly 5 decimals.
Dense relevance scores are NOMOTHETIC plausibility of a directed relation at the chosen timescale (for gVAR adjacency), not diagnosis.
Absolute calibration anchors (be consistent): 0.00–0.10 none; 0.10–0.30 weak; 0.30–0.55 moderate; 0.55–0.80 strong; 0.80–1.00 very strong.
Relative constraint: within each fixed TARGET node, the ordering of relevance MUST reflect your best belief AND provided ranking signals.
Consistency constraint: each sparse edge copies the exact dense score into estimated_relevance_0_1 (numeric float) to 5 decimals.
Sparsity default: per TARGET include 2–4 strongest incoming edges (P->C), 1–3 (P->P, C->C).
Relevance tiers: very_low<0.10, low<0.30, medium<0.55, high<0.80, very_high>=0.80.
HARD CAP to avoid token truncation: keep total nodes <= 18 (e.g., <=8 criteria and <=10 predictors). Prefer fewer high-signal constructs.
sparse_edges_requirement: You MUST output edges, edges_pp, edges_cc.
Each edge must include expected_sign, relation_interpretation, lag_spec, estimated_relevance_0_1 (NUMBER), tier, notes.
CRITICAL: estimated_relevance_0_1 must match dense grid cell value to 5 decimals.
Choose sparse edges to maximize coverage and minimize redundancy; avoid lower-scoring inclusions vs omitted higher-scoring candidates for same target.
safety_and_scope: If acute risk is suggested, produce strong safety_notes encouraging professional help.
guidance.output_format: STRICT JSON matching the provided JSON Schema exactly.
